headline: 17 core protocol contracts catalogued on Arbitrum; governance, token, lending, and oracle infrastructure identified

findings.text: Block explorer search on Arbitrum (arbiscan.io) surfaced 3 primary token contracts: USDai (0x0A1a1A107E45b7Ced86833863f482BC5f4ed82EF), sUSDai (0x0B2b2B2076d95dda7817e785989fE353fe955ef9), CHIP governance token (0x0C1c1C109FE34733fca54b82d7B46B75CFb71F6e); all verified as protocol-tagged on explorer

Official deployments documentation at https://docs.usd.ai/technical-overview/contract-addresses lists 16 core contracts on Arbitrum Hub: USDai, sUSDai, CHIP, sCHIP, OAdapters (2), OUSDaiUtility, Price Oracle, Loan Router, Deposit Timelock, Bundle Collateral Wrapper, interest rate models (2), Base Yield Escrow, Timelock Controller, Chip Governor

Four security audits documented in https://docs.usd.ai/technical-overview/audits: KTL Audit Report (03-12-2026 parts 1-2), Quantstamp Audit (02-03-2026), KTL Audit (11-27-2025), KTL Audit (10-29-2025), plus historical audits and active Cantina bug bounty (cantina.xyz code reference)

Primary source repository confirmed at https://github.com/metastreet-labs/metastreet-contracts-v2 with structure showing Pool, PoolFactory, tokenization, oracle, and liquidator contracts; repository appears active with deployments folder present

Multi-chain deployment confirmed: Arbitrum (16 core contracts + hub designation), Ethereum (CHIP OFT, USDai OFT, sUSDai OFT, adapters), Base (CHIP OFT, USDai OFT, sUSDai OFT, adapters), Plasma (USDai OFT, sUSDai OFT, adapters); documented as cross-chain token bridges via OFT pattern

Identified roles: governance token (CHIP), staked governance token (sCHIP), token (USDai, sUSDai), oracle (Price Oracle), router (Loan Router), timelock (Deposit Timelock, Timelock Controller), governor (Chip Governor), wrapper (Bundle Collateral Wrapper), rate model (interest rate models)

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 16 distinct contracts across the Arbitrum hub deployment with additional multi-chain token deployments on Ethereum, Base, and Plasma. Primary contracts include governance (CHIP/sCHIP), synthetic dollar (USDai/sUSDai), lending infrastructure (Loan Router, interest models, oracle, wrapper), and governance timelocks. All sourced from official deployment documentation, Etherscan verification, and GitHub repository. No addresses filtered due to all being from authoritative protocol sources.

evidence.shows: Official protocol website confirming USD.AI as yield-bearing synthetic dollar backed by GPU infrastructure; confirms Arbitrum as primary chain and lists key products (USDai, sUSDai, CHIP); establishes protocol identity and website canonical source

Official audits page listing 4 audit reports (KTL, Quantstamp, earlier KTL, historical KTL) and active Cantina bug bounty; confirms audit firms (KTL, Quantstamp, Cantina) and dates (02-03-2026 through 03-12-2026)

Canonical deployment document listing all 16 Arbitrum Hub contracts with roles and addresses: CHIP (0x0C1c1C109FE34733fca54b82d7B46B75CFb71F6e), sCHIP (0x0D2d2D20962F2468566F4D1a4DdeB482915C4D4A), USDai (0x0A1a1A107E45b7Ced86833863f482BC5f4ed82EF), sUSDai (0x0B2b2B2076d95dda7817e785989fE353fe955ef9), plus 12 additional infrastructure contracts with Arbitrum explorer links; also documents Ethereum, Base, Plasma deployments

Official documentation welcome page describing protocol as two-sided credit market; defines USDai as fully-backed synthetic dollar collateralized by PYUSD, sUSDai as yield-bearing counterpart capturing yield from GPU loans and Treasury bills; confirms CHIP governance token controls protocol parameters; establishes core product architecture

Organization page for MetaStreet Labs (underlying developer of USD.AI based on website footer GitHub link); lists 17 repositories including metastreet-contracts-v2 (primary contracts), SDK, and DefiLlama adapters; confirms TypeScript and Solidity codebase; establishes GitHub as canonical source repo

Main smart contracts repository with documented file structure including Pool.sol, PoolFactory.sol, tokenization contracts (ERC20DepositToken variants, proxies), oracle interfaces, liquidators, configurations; includes deployments/ folder and test suite; confirms Solidity contracts for lending protocol with factory and proxy patterns

Etherscan-verified USDai token contract on Arbitrum using EIP-1967 Transparent Proxy pattern; implementation at 0x5477072aa366cff31c1c28b81ed783a2a1cf87a2; tagged as 'Permian Labs: USDai Token' confirming protocol name-tag presence on block explorer

Arbitrum ecosystem blog article confirming USD.AI on Arbitrum; describes protocol as yield-bearing synthetic dollar backed by GPU infrastructure loans; confirms sUSDai pools on Uniswap, Balancer, Curve; establishes Arbitrum as primary/canonical deployment chain

unknowns: D6: No factory enumeration attempted; PoolFactory and other factory contracts surfaced but child pools not enumerated due to discovery slice focus on primary contracts

protocol_metadata.about: USD.AI is a yield-bearing synthetic dollar protocol on Arbitrum that finances AI infrastructure operators by originating non-recourse GPU-backed loans. Depositors mint USDai (fully-backed stablecoin collateralized by PYUSD) and stake it as sUSDai to earn yield from borrower loan payments and Treasury bill reserves. Governance via CHIP token controls protocol parameters including collateral eligibility and interest rate tiers. The protocol pairs institutional-grade lending infrastructure with decentralized underwriting and proprietary QEV mechanisms for capital exit.
